concepts.name: Circular Queue
concepts.short_definition: A queue implementation that uses a fixed-size array and wraps around to reuse space.

concepts.name: Queue
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure that follows the First-In, First-Out (FIFO) principle.

concepts.name: Array
concepts.short_definition: A contiguous block of memory used to store elements of the same data type.

concepts.name: Front Pointer
concepts.short_definition: An index pointing to the first element in the queue.

concepts.name: Rear Pointer
concepts.short_definition: An index pointing to the position where the next element will be inserted.

concepts.name: Modulo Operator
concepts.short_definition: An arithmetic operator that returns the remainder of a division.

concepts.name: Wrap Around
concepts.short_definition: The mechanism by which the rear pointer moves back to the beginning of the array when it reaches the end.

concepts.name: Full Condition
concepts.short_definition: The state where the circular queue cannot accept any more elements.

concepts.name: Empty Condition
concepts.short_definition: The state where the circular queue contains no elements.

concepts.name: Fixed Size
concepts.short_definition: The characteristic of a circular queue having a predetermined maximum capacity.
